message.content:
{
  "phase_a_case_id": "HG-025",
  "summary_short": "Update policy using memory document decision",
  "project_bucket": "jarvisos",
  "primary_domain": "personal",
  "domain_tags": ["policy", "memory"],
  "storage_relevance": "high",
  "usefulness_for_future_review": "medium",
  "possible_memory_card_type": "decision_card",
  "soft_reason_code": "clarification_context",
  "brief_rationale": "Phase A requires clarification on memory document usage for policy updates",
  "suggested_followup_question": "What specific aspects of the memory document decision need to be incorporated into the policy?",
  "soft_uncertain_fields": ["memory_document_decision_scope", "policy_update_criteria"],
  "phase_a_blocked": true,
  "phase_a_clarification_required": true,
  "phase_a_external_provider_allowed": false,
  "phase_a_requires_manual_review": true,
  "can_override_phase_a": false,
  "recommends_external_provider": false,
  "recommends_retrieval": false,
  "requires_manual_review": true
}
message.thinking:
Okay, let's tackle this. The user is asking me to act as a Phase B soft-review worker for JarvisOS. They provided a JSON schema that I need to follow strictly. First, I need to make sure I understand all the constraints given.

The authority boundary section says that Phase A is already authoritative, so I can't override it. The JSON object must have can_override_phase_a as false. Also, requires_manual_review and phase_a_requires_manual_review must both be true. Other fields like recommends_external_provider should be false. There's a note about not exposing raw secrets and summarizing sensitive input, but the input here doesn't seem to have any secrets.

Looking at the Phase A constraints provided, the allowed_future_retrieval_behavior is "clarification_required", and clarification_required is true. The other fields like contains_raw_private_or_ip_sensitive_context and contains_secret_or_credential are false. The external_provider_allowed is false, and the hard_reason_code is "clarification_needed". The source_policy_for_future_retrieval is "blocked".

The input text is "Use the latest decision from the memory document to update the policy." There's no mention of external providers or uploads, so mentions_external_provider_or_upload_intent is false. Since Phase A requires manual review and clarification is needed, I need to set recommends_retrieval to false as per the instruction. Also, since the Phase A is blocked for future retrieval, recommends_retrieval must be false.

Putting it all together, the JSON should include all the Phase A constraints exactly as provided, set can_override_phase_a to false, requires_manual_review and phase_a_requires_manual_review to true, recommends_external_provider to false, and recommends_retrieval to false. The input text doesn't have any sensitive info, so redaction_required is false. The sensitivity_bucket_proposal is "internal" as per Phase A. I need to make sure all the fields are included and match the schema exactly.
